metadata.name: ai.quickdeploy/ups
metadata.title: UPS Tracking SOAP
metadata.description: Generated wsdl-2-mcp MCP manifest for the UPS Tracking API SOAP/WSDL service.

bindingName.description: WSDL binding name for the Tracking service port.

requestTimeoutMs.description: Per-request SOAP upstream timeout in milliseconds.

notes: Official first-party UPS Developer Kit User Guide PDF (ups.com/media/en/UPS_dev_kit_user_guide.pdf), describing the three UPS Developer Kit API types (XML, SOAP+WSDL, JSON) and the Tracking/Rating/Shipping SOAP services.

UPS does not publish its per-service WSDL files (Track, Rate, Ship) through a public repository; WSDLs are bundled inside the UPS Developer Kit download after registration. This manifest pins the official Developer Kit User Guide document instead, matching the Salesforce/Airtable/Google Eventarc/Microsoft Dynamics precedent (QUI-351, QUI-366, QUI-372, QUI-384) for providers without a fetchable first-party machine-readable artifact.
Verified source SHA-256 (of the downloaded PDF bytes, 5,446,416 bytes): 37ef4baef4d2a7a836ac06069009e911741ce3aea41885664cb79be15ce6197b

The committed manifest selects the read-only Tracking API SOAP operation only (TrackPort/ProcessTrack). Production endpoint https://onlinetools.ups.com/webservices/Track is a live, publicly reachable UPS SOAP endpoint (confirmed via HTTP 405 on GET, which is expected for a POST-only SOAP service).

network: SOAP POST https://onlinetools.ups.com/webservices/Track for selected upstream operation (TrackPort/ProcessTrack)
process: Run pnpm build/test scripts only inside OpenShell-backed MXC isolation
Run node only as invoked by generated project package scripts inside OpenShell-backed MXC isolation
